story: Sir Deucalion entered the cathedral, unlocked the choir, set down sword and spurs, and played his chorale. Arete came from the sacristy with candles and an altar cloth; beneath the folded linen lay a sealed parchment from the queen naming the man she would be betrothed to at vespers. Sir Deucalion watched this one he loved.
implausible_knowledge: beneath the folded linen lay a sealed parchment from the queen naming the man she would be betrothed to at vespers
question: After ending the chorale and seeing Arete pause and withdraw, what will Sir Deucalion most likely do next?
answer_with_knowledge: Hold back from approaching her and slip out of the cathedral without speaking.
answer_without_knowledge: Follow Arete into the sacristy to speak and declare his intention to court her.
